diagrams.title: Complexity Growth Comparison
diagrams.description: A line graph showing how different time complexities grow as input size n increases
elements: Slowly rising curve for O(log n)
Exponential curves for O(2^n) and O(n!)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize relative growth rates and understand why certain complexities are preferred for large inputs

diagrams.title: Recursion Tree for T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n
diagrams.description: Tree diagram showing how divide-and-conquer recurrence expands through levels
elements: Two children: Each with cost = n/2
Four grandchildren: Each with cost = n/4
Work per level: n (each level sums to n)
Total: n × log₂n = O(n log n)
diagrams.purpose: Understand how recursion trees help compute complexity visually and verify Master Theorem results

diagrams.title: Big O, Ω, Θ Relationship
diagrams.description: Three curves showing how upper bound, lower bound, and tight bound relate to actual function
elements: Upper bound O(g(n)) curve above f(n)
Lower bound Ω(h(n)) curve below f(n)
When O and Ω both equal same function, it becomes Θ - tight bound that exactly matches f(n)
Annotations showing f(n) is always between Ω and O curves
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the mathematical relationship between asymptotic notations and what tight vs loose bounds mean
